source-discipline.reason: Inheritance candidates derived by exact-string match on standard_number across NULL-verification rows. The same-edition constraint is explicit. No new external probes; inheritance writes reference the canonical row whose probe is logged in prior session writes.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: Inheritance mechanic extends rule #10's existence-axis writes to duplicate-standard rows. The constraint (exact standard_number match) preserves rule #10's verification meaning per row — inheriting rows do not claim independent verification, they claim equivalence to a canonical verification. Net +10 to eligible pool from this mechanic without new external probes.

bias_direction: Inheritance is a yield-multiplier mechanic introduced AFTER the cumulative 3-batch yield results (5/14 = 36% gate-clear) showed slower-than-projected progress. The bias risk is toward inheritance over-reaching — claiming verification status for rows that may differ in edition or scope from the canonical even when standard_number strings match. The exact-string-match constraint mitigates this but does not eliminate it. Two rows can have the same standard_number value and still differ in pub_year, in scope-of-citation, or in which clause is being cited. The verification_note must explicitly reference the canonical so the audit trail catches mismatches when they surface.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would object that inheritance is a procedural shortcut that defeats the point of per-row verification. Why not just deduplicate same-standard rows entirely, like the Bettarello dedup earlier in this session? Counter: the project's slug-based citation model deliberately allows the same source to be cited under different slug-and-scope contexts (per the KNOWN_DUP_DOIS allowlist in test_db_integrity D01 — IEC 60118-4 triple, DIN 18040-2 five-section citations, etc.). Same-standard same-edition is NOT a dedup signal; it is a multi-section citation signal. The right operation is to inherit status across the multi-section citations, not collapse them into one row. The inheritance mechanic respects the project's intentional same-source-multiple-citations pattern.
